title: Executive Order 9595—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the United States Rubber Company, Located in Or Around Detroit, Michigan

flags.justification: Broad seizure and operating authority, meaningfully bounded by the protective requirements in Secs. 2 and 3.
Sec. 5 sets a concrete 60-day cap after productive efficiency is restored.
Single purpose: seizure of this one company's plants.
Sec. 2 requires operation 'in accordance with the terms and conditions of employment which are in effect at the time possession thereof is taken.'

Sec. 4 directs the FBI to 'immediately undertake an investigation of any matter affecting the operation of said plants' upon the Secretary of War's request — an open-ended investigative trigger with no defined standard, distinct from the other seizure orders in this batch, creating latent potential for investigative involvement in labor-relations matters beyond straightforward plant security.
The 60-day cap in Sec. 5 is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap and status-quo-employment-terms requirement provide real, checkable constraints on the core seizure authority; the FBI-investigation concern is captured under Flag 5.

The FBI-investigation-on-request clause (Sec. 4) is a new feature not present in sibling seizure orders in this corpus, representing a template expansion that normalizes FBI involvement in labor-dispute-adjacent seizures.
Sec. 3's management-preservation clause and Sec. 4's cooperation directive are protective, anti-conflict design features.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 3/20 = 15.0%. Otherwise near-identical to EO 9593, distinguished by the added open-ended FBI investigative clause.
